column15:
Dr Moukhaiber is an amazing attending, her rounds in the PICU are organized and thorough.
She is always accessible and available to answer our queries and to address our concerns.

Dr. Majdalani is a wonderful attending who shows a lot of concern for the patients and the staff. 
Plans are always made for the patient's benefit during her rounds in the early morning.
She support us in every circumstance, pays attention to our issues, responds to them, and works to find solutions.
Dr Ismail is always present when a support is needed.

Dr Sbaity's conduct can be borderline unprofessional, if not downright disrespectful and degrading at times. Working with her is emotionally draining. She is a good academic and a good surgeon, if only her attitude and conduct with the residents could be improved.
We are lucky to have Dr Khoury on board and it is a great privilege to learn from his expertise.

Great skills.
Enjoy teaching medical students, residents and fellows.

Respectful.
An excellent physician with a great knowledge.

Respectful. 
Enthusiastic about physiology.
Loves to teach others.

Dr Haddad is a professional vascular surgeon who works responsibly with all his patients, follows up well on them, respects all the staff, appreciates our work. I have been working with him for several years and I am impressed by his attitude and work.

Dr Hoballah is a highly professional vascular surgeon and chairman who works responsibly with all his patients, follows up well on them pre and post operatively, respects all the staff, appreciates our work. I have been working with him for several years and I am impressed by his attitude and work.
Such an amazing character, highly professional, a role model, working with Dr. Khouzami is always a pleasure. He respects nurses / nurses workflow. Highly cooperative.

Such a talented physician with great knowledge and communication skills, he is the number 1 attending physician according to my perspective. He is the only physician who teaches us about patient cases and discusses the treatment plan with us. Proud to work with Dr Bassem.

Role model
A pleasure to learn from
Residents are fortunate to have her as their program director

Dr Tanios has shown himself to be a great physician, nephrologist, patient–advocate and mentor/educator.
He has wonderful beside manners, listens and help patients with all their needs, always conducts himelf in utmost professionalism.
A true pleasure to have Bassem as a member of the nephrology team

Dr Daouk is simply a great senior physician, patient–advocate and mentor. She takes it personally upon herself to assist patients in need of help, be it by advocating for them, supporting request for helping them or raising funds for the Kidney Patient fund from donors. We are all indebted to her efforts and consistent advocacy for all patients.
She is a wonderful mentor to fellows. She is highly liked and respected by her patients and by staff alike.

Dr Mallat is a very experienced senior nephrologist and internist, a true academician and role mode. He maintains a very busy practice, attending to all its needs and demands, and providing fisrt–rate medical care and service to patients. He is highly respected as a nephrologist, as a medical staff member, as a faculty member with seniority and simply as a trusted colleague. He participates and enriches all the activies of the Division and plays an active role in the educational mission of the Division.

Dr Medawwar is a seasoned, highly experienced, senior nephrologist and internist. He maintains a busy high volume practice to which he devotes all necessary efforts, meeting needs of patients at all times. He is a wonderful physician, person and colleague who is highluy respected by patients and staff alike. He is always at the ready to help. He conducts himself in most professional manner.

I have providing renal care in the inpatient setting through consultations and as Admitting Physician, in the outpatient clinic at ACC, in the Mamdouha el–Sayed Bobst Hemodialysis Unit, maintaining an active busy regional practice. I have also been providing administrative support for the outpatient and inpatient dialysis program, CRRT, Peritoneal Dialysis, Hypertension lab among other services. This is in addition to coaching and mentoring of fellows, residents and students. I have also worked closely with Dr Daouk to administer the Patient Support funds and aid those in need.
Dr. Hamza is highly knowledgeable and delicated to patients, respect all staff members and always follow up about patients conditions and treatments
A great respectful surgeon and mentor, dedicated to teaching and patient care.
despite his busy schedule, he keeps up with his clinical and research work within the highest standards of ethics, respect to staff, dedication to teaching and patient care.

despite being overloaded with clinical work and administrative one, he does his best to accommodate between all tasks within his limits. Staff and residents know him very well that he is very serious when it deals with work and patient care and that it has nothing to do with friendships. Patients love him and get only positive feedback from them.
Dr. Moukaddam is a role model.
Dr. Muallem is a role model.

Thank you for this comprehensive survey. I hope it will be used to provide constructive and professional feedback on my actual behaviour. And I wish that all people that I work with will fill it in an honest way.
I am known to come to the OR before any other employee, to carry the patient and wheel them when escorts are not around. Delaying patients is not in my dictionary

I have known Dr Yazbeck since my appointment at AUBMC spanning over 25 years now..
Dr Yazbeck and myself have shared and continue to share the care of many patients. She has always been welcoming to discussions we do together regarding patient care. She undoubtedly is accommodating to last minute patients to be seen and always with a smile. Echoes from patients I refer to her are positive. We also share work on studies as well as the Nutrition Committee that handles new emerging recommendations and critical care nutrition guidelines as well as highly specialised nutrition support intervention. She is indeed much respected and loved by the medical students and resident community.
Indeed a professional respectful attending–one of the best. I personally look forward to working with her many more years to come

I have worked with Dr Karam for the past years since 1998. Initially I have known her when she used to be consulted for the inherited metabolic cases at AUBMC to then officially join our medical centre. Since then I share with her all patients enrolled in the IMDP program/clinic. I also share with her several publications on our patients. She is someone who welcomes discussions and works diligently to the best of her patients. I do call her anytime to attend to the many patient issues we have regarding the difficult diseases we deal in the metabolic conditions. Moreover we do share a course we teach together at FAFS (medical Nutrition Therapy) and sixths was the first of its kind–a course we designed to be co–taught by her as the medical provider and myself as the Biblical Dietitian. The feed back from the course is amazing. So is the feedback from medical students and residents who deal with her. Dr Karam is indeed a professional, respectful and ethical physician whom I enjoy working with and look forward to continue doing..
This is a self–assessment! at least this is what i believe about myself.

Dr. Khatib is a true leader who has worked conscientiously, efficiently & professionaly to move our department & all our staff to higher level of knowledge & skills based on updated evidence–based science & medical research. Moreover, he raised up our commitment to all job–related issues & strengthen our obligations toward our institutional vision.

Dr. Sbaity is an impolite person, she always crosses her boundaries while communicating with staff. she shouts. she hangs up the phone on our face. she is aggressive and moody. she always gossips on behind of staff with others that staff are useless and do not work.
Good communication skills both with patients and healthcare providers
Best communication skills, professional, and humble.
Dr Bulbul is professional, easy–going, respectful and responsive.
Dr Hajj is professional, easy–going, ethical, respectful and responsive. It is a pleasure to work with him. He displays professionalism and care for the patients, residents, and staff.
Dr Khauli is professional, respectful, and knowledgeable.
Dr Nasr is respectful, professional and responsive.
Dr Wazzan is a respectful, professional and humble doctor. It is a great pleasure to work with him.

Promoting and behaving in a professional and respectful manner at work with patients and colleagues is a core value of mine, which I uphold and practice daily. Being open to concerns and feedback from colleagues as well as sharing my opinions with them is also something I practice with honesty and respect. I see these practices as the basis for a safe and healthy work place environment.
Very fine, compassionate, and ethical eye surgeon.
Very mature, and competent Eye Surgeon.
Very compassionate, mature, and ethical Eye Surgeon.
very knowledgible, ethical, and competent Eye Surgeon.

I tend to maintain eye contact with patients and don't let the EMR take my attention when the patient is with me. 

I give time to explain to patients the cause of symptoms and educate them about treatment options and how to use the suggestefodality, example using inhalers. 

I feel drained with some HIP patients who abuse the system by asking for u indicated sick leaves, referrals, studies.
Dr. Ahmad Zaghal is a very professional physician and faculty member. He always attend to his duties, and treats everyone fairly.
Dr. Eman Sbaity professionalism is questioned sometimes, she need to be informed of this matter to improve her relation with healthcare workers both clinical and non–clinical.
Dr. George Abi Saad is a professional and a genius healthcare provider, he follows up closely with his patients and provides exceptional care.
Very respectful physician and faculty member. Working with him is very smooth and efficient.
Dr. Jabe Abbas needs to seek professional development training, he needs to treat staff workers with more respect and more professionally.
Dr. Mohamad Khalife communicates effectively with his patients, and all staff members. He is a genuine person with a positive character.

Dr. Mayyas Msheik is a professional physician that respects all staff and his fellow colleagues. Working with him is very efficient, however, sometime he needs his matters done right away, he follows–up very closely, and likes his work done right away. Overall, I like working with him.
Dr. Mustapha Sidani is a respectful physician and he appreciate the work of staff.
It is a pleasure working with Dr. Samir Akel, he is a professional physician and a well rounded educator.
Dr. Samer Deeba is a professional Physician and professional. It takes him some time to trust staff, especially if they are new, but overall he works fast and efficiently.

Dr. Samer Doughan is a pleasant physician, he doesn't trust new staff in the beginning but over time after he gets used to them he shows good communication and initiative. Although favoritism of older staff plays part in his judgement sometimes.
It is a pleasure working with Salim Rahhal.

Dr. Noun is one of the best Attending physicians I have encountered throughout my career. She is Humane and treats people as human beings and not as subjects or duty. She is loved and adored by all her patients.

Value patient care and confort 
Respect relationship with healthcare staff
Dr Yunis is very supportive, in fact he listens to everyone and teaches us that patient care comes first. His medical approach is unique and highly professional.

Doctor Boustany, is a true role model of how physicians are expected to think, communicate and act. Either from a professional/academic perspective or from a humanitarian perspective, she is one from whom we can absorb a lot.

Examples of such professional/ humanitarian behaviours include (few examples): 
–Teaching the mother during our rounds on the ward how to properly feed her infant who has severe gastric reflux.
–Always listening to patient's parents (neurological and non–neurological concerns,complaints..etc) and providing them with the solutions instantly with no delay.
–Always on time (whether for clinics, ward rounds, meetings and conferences).  
–Adding extra (early morning) clinic visits for urgent cases 
–Helping thousands of families vi her Open mind funds
And the list goes on and on
Vey professional and educational. Keen eyes for details

meticulous approach to reviewing imagin
interpretations and diagnoses have been consistently accurate and precise, providing invaluable guidance for patient management. 
outstanding care and expertise
Truly inspired by her dedication and expertise
The best! We are so lucky to have him in our program. truly an inspiration, a prodigy!

My unbiased and completely fair feedback about this attending is reflective of what was mentioned above. She is extremely dedicated, loves her job, extremely professional with her patients, families, and all hospital staff. She is always ready to help and answer questions no matter the time. She is highly approachable.
most amazing person you can ever meet

There is a growing sensation of leniency as the prevailing atmosphere of discontent. The other parts are in the same trap, and the frustrations and shortage of healthcare workers are leading to further decompensation. This is contradictory to all the above

he is very respectful , has good communication skills and responsive at all times

Dr. Bassil has built a wonderful reputation, that many of his colleagues have entrusted him with the care of their family members. His is highly praised by fellow workers including physicians and nursing staff.

He is one of the major providers on the palliative care service, mainly driven by his values and medical ethics, and his desire to support patients and families facing challenging medical conditions.
One head nurse referred to Dr. Farah as 'the best attending we are working with"

Concerns has been raised about Dr. Hajjar's availability and satisfaction of patients. The FM leadership developed a plan of close supervision for a period of 6 month to be implemented by the division head with weekly follow up. Dr. Hajjar was very responsive and compliant with the plan. As this period comes to an end, we have not heard any significant complaint from either patients affairs or nursing staff since the plan was implemented. 

It is important to note that Dr. Hajjar is a caring and skilled clinician with an excellent medical knowledge base.

Also, Dr. Hajjar has always been committed and very serious about his ABIM recertification in Geriatric Medicine, Internal Medicine, and Hospice/Palliatice care.
This is a self evaluation. over the last year, i have done less clinical service that i usually do, but i plan to pick this up.

very decent, very respectful and brilliant in hr job
Dr Ramzi Haddad is excellent in everything he does and all levels. humanitarian and professional. I believe every one likes him so much even suppliers

I am writing to provide feedback on my experiences working with Dr. Sbaity. 
During our interactions, I have observed instances where Dr. Sbaity has exhibited unprofessional behavior, including dishonesty and disrespect. It is essential for a medical professional to maintain trust and integrity with patients and colleagues alike, and these behaviors have created a challenging working environment.

To foster a more positive and effective work environment, I would recommend that Dr. Sbaity undergo further training or development in communication skills, empathy, and professionalism. It is important for all team members to uphold the highest standards of ethical conduct and respectful communication.

I appreciate the opportunity to provide this evaluation, and I hope that these concerns can be addressed to ensure a more harmonious and productive work environment.
Effortless attempts to organize work in a healthy environment is not always productive
God bless him, proud to be part of his team
Dr. Mokalled is one in a million she listens to her patients & reassure them when they have concerns, she treats patients with love and respect.

Since he has joined the Department, Elie has been a role model.

Timely and organized.
He communicates with patients in a very serious manner and discloses bad news in a way that is hard for patient and families. Other than that, the plan of care is always clear and defined, he comes to the round early and on time.

Her management is clear, very reasonable and systematic.
However, she comes to round too late, on weekdays and on weekends. 
Patients complain of not being included in her plan of care and that she doesn't educate them or listen to their needs. 
Concerning healthcare colleagues, she's professional and thoughtful, and always tries to educate residents and nurses.
I have never had the chance to work with Dr. Adra
i have never worked with Dr. farah

I am very proud to be an AUBMC physician .I am always doing my best to provide the best patient care while respecting AUBMC principles .
Hopefully I ll keep up to the expectations of this esteemed institution.
Role model. one of the best attending I've ever worked with. respectful, always updated on literature, caring, dedicated to patient's care and to our learning process.

He gives his patients and family members his time to explain about treatment plan and possible side effects. respects the suggestions and concerns of other healthcare workers, and I have witnessed this personally on several occasions. Patients and family members leave the clinic/infusion center well educated about the plan of care.

she contacts her patients after each test result to reassure them or discuss plan of care which keeps her patients content and satisfied.

Every time i see dr noun pass by the unit i see her with a smile on her face. She is very respectful and mindful of the patients and their parents and she addresses all their concerns while providing them with clear and realistic expectations of the plan of care. She is also very respectful of the units staff and is mindful not to interrupt the nurses if they are working on the patient while she is rounding.
Dr. Bassem is a role model of a physician.

Dr. aouad is the best chairperson that have ever been appointed in the anesthesia department. She is extremely polite and professional and cooperative with all staff in the operating rooms and the hospital. She is a role model for all anesthesiologists in her level of knowledge and hand skills in all fields of anesthesia.

Dr. Khalili is one of the best anesthesiologists that have ever been appointed in the anesthesia department. His professional attitude and excellent communication skills with all of the nursing staff and anesthesia residents is an example for being a role model in all aspects. We highly respect him and respect his level of anesthesia knowledge and hand skills especially in the field of pediatric and adult open heart.

Dr. Yunis is an exceptional physician. He is known for his outstanding clinical skills and his dedication to providing the highest quality of care to his patients. Dr. Yunis is always professional and courteous in his interactions with staff and families, and he is an excellent communicator who takes the time to listen to the parents and discuss their concerns. He is a true team player who is always willing to collaborate with others to ensure the best possible outcomes for his patients. Overall, Dr. Yunis is an outstanding physician who is respected and admired by everyone who has the pleasure of working with him.

despite his busy clinical and administrative work, he does his best to accommodate all tasks within his limits. staff and residents acknowledge his seriousness at work and when dealing with patient care without affecting friendships outside work. his patients love him and gets only positive feedback from them

Very occasionally we have parents of mentally challenged/ chronic or autistic children that are  irritating and vindictive to the nursing and pediatric and surgical  staff that we all have  to cope up with ( and each of us ventilate separately ) during their management.

Always tries to teach the fellows during the tumor boards, outpatient rounds and clinic schedule.
Does not intimidate the fellows, treats them with respect.

Gives appropriate time to all his patients
Teaches the fellows during the clinic schedule, tumor boards, and inpatient rotations
Shows interest in the fellows, asks about their future plans and goals

Great mentor and teacher
Takes time to teach the fellows during the clinics exposure
Respectful and kind towards patients

Teaches the fellows and other team members (interns, residents...) during inpatient rounds
Teaches the fellows mainly during the weekly leukemia/lymphoma rounds
Punctual in his inpatient round timing, efficient in making decisions, explains clearly the situation to the patients no matter how complicated they might be

However, he puts pressure on the fellows on things that are not their responsibility (e.g. he asks the fellows to ask the oncology pharmacists to stay after hours to prepare chemotherapy despite knowing that they are understaffed and all the senior pharmacists already quit the hospital; asks for bone marrow results in 24h even when he knows that the hospital only has 1 heme–pathologist and might take him days to reach the slides of that particular patient...)
Sometimes can be hurtful in his comments even when they are made as a joke

In my humble opinion, he does not give adequate time for inpatients and their families.
He completely relies on fellows to see his new patients and fill their histories (e.g. might cancel his clinic when he doesn't have a fellow, or adds several drop in new cases whenever a fellow is assigned with him that same day). Nevertheless, he teaches the fellows during the tumor boards and clinic rotations.

He never stops helping the patients in any way he can (providing free medications...), Nevertheless:

– Does not give completely accurate information to the patients, which in turn might affect their decision (e.g. tells a patient who needs stem cell transplant that transplant related mortality is 1.5% when in fact it reaches 20%).
– Talks to medical team members/nurses in a demeaning way (even if he's saying the words as a joke, they are hurtful) during the inpatient specially on Tuesdays when he's the only attending rounding. For instance, he tells patients that the students only care about going to the beach and don't pay attention to patient care. He once told a patient he saw for the first time that the fellow rotating with them will take her stem cells and sell them in the black market, and the patient burst into tears. He makes occasional jokes about fat people and those from different cultures, and the list goes on.
– He tells the patients that the management of their case (by his colleagues in the same team) are completely wrong even when it's not.
– He once agreed to treat a patient without telling her she has cancer, forcing us (the fellows) to lie to her when consenting her for chemotherapy or bone marrow biopsy.
– He gives himself credit for things he did not do.
– He changes his mind when the decision he took impacts the patients negatively, and denies taking that decision in the first place and blames the team.
– He insists on his decisions despite knowing that they might hurt the patients: for instance a well educated patient with 17p deletion Multiple Myeloma (high risk) pentarefractory had cord compression because of major disease progression. He refused to tell the patient or his family how serious the situation is, and insisted on operating on the patient instead of giving systemic treatment. He eventually changed his mind, but the patient deteriorated and got intubated and transferred to the ICU because of the delay in management.

He has a good heart, but unfortunately lacks communication skills and good clinical sense.

She is an exemplary human and physician in terms of knowledge and patient care, however:

– Sometimes, she replies late to our messages (waits until finishes her clinic), which might affect patient care negatively
– She frequently changes her mind about decisions she already took on patients (chemotherapy, transplant, admission, discharge..), which frustrates us and the whole team

Average waiting time in clinics range between 1.5–2.5 hours, which makes fellows subject to disputes with patients because of the long waiting time 
Relatively harder to reach compared to other physicians

Exemplary human and physician by all means, always ready to listen to the patients and the fellows when needed
Flexible to ease the rotations/duties of the fellows (e.g. doesn't mind rounding on her own on Saturdays when the fellow overstays in the round with another physician...)

Dr sawaya is very knowledgeable, fellows in cardiology department learn a lot from him, his experience and his skills make him one of the best interventional cardiologist in the region. He doesn’t dedicate time for teaching but he always shares information and knowledge during work and during his cases in the cath lab.

He is not someone you can learn anything from, he is nice and good with his patients but doesn’t share any knowledge with his fellows, he has a very rigid mind, doesn’t accept any new ideas or any change for the cardiology department to be better.

There is a huge gap in communication between Dr refaat and his fellows, knowing that he is the program director of cardiology division, he is never available, he doesn’t seem to care about his fellows, no educational activities, no access to any cardiology platforms, no local or international lectures invitations, he doesn’t do anything in the benefit of his program, in conclusion he doesn’t fit his title at all.

Based on my observations, patients' dissatisfaction is a growing hidden problem within AUBMC. 
While it is true that the number of patients seen is often considered an important metric in healthcare settings, it is essential to prioritize both quantity and quality of care for optimal patient outcomes. Focusing solely on the number of patients seen can potentially compromise the quality and safety of healthcare delivery.
Patients' feedback and satisfaction are crucial for the image and reputation of the hospital. Feedback from inpatients and outpatients provides valuable insights into the quality of care and services provided by the hospital, allowing the hospital administration to identify areas for improvement and enhance the overall patient experience.
